rationale: Swain et al. is cited in core framing sections as one of the few studies that examined mechanisms of misresponse, and the authors position their contribution as addressing limits in that prior treatment. The citing paper also discusses Swain et al.'s adaptation of sentence-comprehension logic to surveys and notes that its own Study 1 uses scales similar to Swain et al. (2008), showing design-level influence. This is therefore more than a passing background citation because it helps define both the gap and the specific advancement claimed.
relation_description: The 2018 paper explicitly builds on Swain et al. (2008) as prior work on misresponse to reversed Likert items, including its initial mechanism analysis and related scale design. It extends that line by introducing a broader typology (negation, polar opposite, reversal), mapping these to inattention versus difficulty mechanisms, and testing them with new modeling and eye-tracking evidence.
